prose.overview: Riverdale Golf Club is one of Bangkok's most respected modern layouts — a 7,006-yard, par-72 course that opened in 2010 in Pathum Thani, just 30 minutes north of the city centre and adjacent to Bangkok Golf Club. Designed by Jonathan Morrow and Al Tikkanen, the course is operated by the MBK Group (the Thai retail and hospitality conglomerate) to a five-star standard, with elegant locker rooms, attentive service, and a conditioning level that rivals anything in the Bangkok region. The design philosophy favours the mid-handicapper who wants genuine challenge without arbitrary difficulty: gently rolling fairways, reed-lined lakes, and raised greens that demand precision without punishing every stray shot. Riverdale attracts a loyal following among Bangkok's expat golfing community and features regularly in Top 10 Bangkok course rankings.
prose.layout_and_experience: Morrow and Tikkanen's design makes artful use of the relatively flat north Bangkok terrain by introducing gentle elevation changes to fairways and approaches, creating a sense of movement rarely felt on Bangkok-area courses. Reed-lined lakes run alongside multiple holes and come directly into play on the more dramatic risk/reward holes, while strategically placed bunkering frames tee-shot landing zones without making the course feel pinched. The signature par-5 6th hole is widely regarded as one of the best in Bangkok — a sweeping, reachable par-5 that tempts aggressive players to attack the lake-guarded green in two. The par-3 8th is another standout, requiring a precise mid-iron to a water-surrounded island green. Greens are of good size, moderately undulating, and hold well for correctly struck approaches. From the back tees at 7,001 yards, the course tests even scratch golfers; from the 5,880-yard forward tees, it plays as an enjoyable challenge for higher handicappers. Golf carts are compulsory throughout, which keeps pace of play brisk. The clubhouse facilities, including restaurants serving Thai cuisine, are well above average.
prose.tips: Reserve tee times at least 2 days in advance on weekdays and at least 5 days for weekends and public holidays — the course is consistently popular. Groups of 3 or more are required before 11:00 AM on weekends. Green fees of approximately 2,700 THB are supplemented by mandatory caddie (400 THB) and cart (800 THB) fees, bringing the total to around 3,900 THB per person. Budget a further 400 THB for the caddie tip. Collared shirts and golf-standard trousers or shorts required. The course is closed for full maintenance on one weekday per month — confirm availability when booking. Club hire at 1,200 THB is reasonable. Travel north on Vibhavadi Rangsit Road or use the expressway to Tiwanon Road for the most direct route from central Bangkok.
prose.location_and_access: Riverdale Golf Club is located at 123/5 Moo 1, Tiwanon Road, Bang Kadi, Mueang Pathum Thani, Pathum Thani 12000 — approximately 30 km north of central Bangkok. The drive from Sukhumvit takes around 30 minutes off-peak via the Don Mueang Expressway (Toll Road No. 5). The club sits beside Bangkok Golf Club on Tiwanon Road, a well-known golf corridor. Taxis and Grab cars are plentiful. No practical public transport serves the club directly.
prose.rental_cta_context: LENGOLF delivers premium golf club sets directly to Bangkok hotels, making it effortless to combine a Riverdale Golf Club tee time with a club rental — no airport check-in fees and no heavy bags.

en.title: Riverdale Golf Club — Green Fees, Course Guide & Golf Club Rentals
en.meta_description: Riverdale Golf Club Pathum Thani green fees, course overview by Jonathan Morrow & Al Tikkanen, tips for visiting, and golf club rentals delivered to your Bangkok hotel.

verification_notes: Designer and year 2010 confirmed by multiple sources. GPS coordinates are model estimates. 1golf.eu shows 4,100 THB flat (likely all-in); golfbangkok shows 2,700 THB green fee + separate caddie 400 + cart 800. Itemized breakdown used.
disputes: 1golf.eu lists 4,100 THB flat vs golfbangkok 2,700 THB green fee only. Likely same total presented differently.
